description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: private Catholic university in Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: DePaul University is a private Catholic research university in Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: American football player (1942–2023)
description_wiki: Richard Marvin Butkus (December 9, 1942 – October 5, 2023) was an American professional football linebacker, sports commentator, and actor.

description: naval training base for the United States Navy, outside of Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: Naval Station Great Lakes (NAVSTA Great Lakes) is the home of the United States Navy's only current boot camp, located near North Chicago, in Lake County, Illinois, along Lake Michigan.

description: city in and county seat of Spokane County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Spokane (  spoh-KAN; Spokane: sƛ̓x̌etkʷ) is a city in and the county seat of Spokane County in the U.S. state of Washington.

description: American diplomat and activist, First Lady of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1884–1962)
description_wiki: Anna Eleanor Roosevelt ( EL-in-or ROH-zə-velt; October 11, 1884 – November 7, 1962) was an American political figure, diplomat, and activist.

description: lake in Kootenai and Bonner counties in Idaho, United States
description_wiki: Lake Pend Oreille ( POND-ə-RAY) in the northern Idaho Panhandle is the largest lake in the U.S. state of Idaho and the 38th-largest lake by area in the United States, with a surface area of 148 square miles (380 km2).

description: American singer and actor (1903–1977)
description_wiki: Harry Lillis "Bing" Crosby Jr.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: neighborhood of San Diego, California, United States
description_wiki: Point Loma (Spanish: Punta de la Loma, meaning "Hill Point"; Kumeyaay: Amat Kunyily, meaning "Black Earth") is a seaside community in San Diego, California, United States.

aliases: Saint Paul, Ramsey, Minnesota, United States
description: capital of the state of Minnesota, United States; county seat of Ramsey County, Minnesota
description_wiki: Saint Paul (often abbreviated to St.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: 1935 Mahan-class destroyer
description_wiki: USS Case (DD-370) was a Mahan-class destroyer in the United States Navy before and during World War II. She was the second ship named for Augustus Ludlow Case.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Alaska (  ə-LASS-kə) is a non-contiguous U.S. state located in the northwestern regions of North America.

description: archipelago in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Hawaiian Islands (Hawaiian: Mokupuni Hawaiʻi) are an archipelago of eight major volcanic islands, several atolls, and numerous smaller islets in the North Pacific Ocean, extending some 1,500 miles (2,400 kilometers) from the island of Hawaiʻi in the south to northernmost Kure Atoll.

aliases: The bridge that couldn't be built
description: suspension bridge on the San Francisco Bay
description_wiki: The Golden Gate Bridge is a suspension bridge spanning the Golden Gate, the one-mile-wide (1.6 km) strait connecting San Francisco Bay and the Pacific Ocean in California, United States.

description: country near the equator in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Marshall Islands, officially the Republic of the Marshall Islands, is an island country in Oceania.

description: atoll of the Marshall Islands
capital of the Marshall Islands
description_wiki: Majuro (; Marshallese: Mājro [mʲæzʲ(e)rˠo]) is the capital and largest city of the Marshall Islands.

description: Pacific atoll, part of the Marshall Islands
description_wiki: Kwajalein Atoll (; Marshallese: Kuwajleen [kʷuwɑzʲ(ɛ)lʲɛːnʲ]) is part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands (RMI).

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: family name
description_wiki: Howe is an English surname with Scandinavian roots.

description: island in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: New Guinea (Tok Pisin: Niugini; Hiri Motu: Niu Gini; Indonesian: Papua, fossilized Nugini, also known as Papua or historically Irian) is the world's second-largest island, with an area of 785,753 km2 (303,381 sq mi).

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: King Neptune may refer to:

description: World War II Japanese submarine class
archipelago in western North Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Mariana Islands ( MARR-ee-AH-nə; Chamorro: Manislan Mariånas), also simply the Marianas, are a crescent-shaped archipelago comprising the summits of fifteen longitudinally oriented, mostly dormant volcanic mountains in the north-western Pacific Ocean, between the 12th and 21st parallels north and along the 145th meridian east.

description: Pacific island, part of the Northern Mariana Islands, United States
unincorporated territory of the United States of America
description_wiki: Guam (  GWAHM; Chamorro: Guåhan [ˈɡʷɑhɑn]) is an island that is an organized, unincorporated territory of the United States in the Micronesia subregion of the western Pacific Ocean.

description: largest island of the Northern Mariana Islands, United States of America
archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

description: 1944 World War II Pacific naval battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Philippine Sea was a major naval battle of World War II on 19–20 June 1944 that eliminated the Imperial Japanese Navy's ability to conduct large-scale carrier actions.

description: 1940 Yamato-class battleship
description_wiki: Yamato (Japanese: 大和; named after the ancient Yamato Province) was the lead ship of her class of battleships built for the Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) shortly before World War II. She and her sister ship, Musashi, were the heaviest and most powerfully armed battleships ever constructed, displacing nearly 72,000 tonnes (71,000 long tons) at full load and armed with nine 46 cm (18.1 in) Type 94 main guns, which were the largest guns ever mounted on a warship.

description: strait connecting Leyte Gulf and Bohol Sea in the Philippines
description_wiki: Surigao Strait (Filipino: Kipot ng Surigaw) is a strait in the southern Philippines, between the Bohol Sea and Leyte Gulf of the Philippine Sea.

description: 1944 World War II Pacific naval battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Philippine Sea was a major naval battle of World War II on 19–20 June 1944 that eliminated the Imperial Japanese Navy's ability to conduct large-scale carrier actions.

description: 1945 major battle in which US Forces captured the island of Iwo Jima from Japan during World War II
description_wiki: The Battle of Iwo Jima (硫黄島の戦い, Iōtō no Tatakai, Iōjima no Tatakai; 19 February – 26 March 1945) was a major battle in which the United States Marine Corps (USMC) and United States Navy (USN) landed on and eventually captured the island of Iwo Jima from the Imperial Japanese Army (IJA) during World War II. The American invasion, designated Operation Detachment, had the goal of capturing the island with its two airfields: South Field and Central Field.

description: Main US Navy strike force, January 1944 - August 1945
aerial warfare branch of the United States Army from 1941 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF or AAF) was the major land-based aerial warfare service component of the United States Army and de facto aerial warfare service branch of the United States during and immediately after World War II (1941–1947).

description: 1945 major battle of the Pacific War
description_wiki: The Battle of Okinawa (Japanese: 沖縄戦, Hepburn: Okinawa-sen), codenamed Operation Iceberg, was a major battle of the Pacific War fought on the island of Okinawa by the United States Army and United States Marine Corps forces against the Imperial Japanese Army.

description: the geographical description of Tokyo's Ogasawara Subprefecture, including the Bonin and Volcano island groups along with three isolated islands, chiefly as a calque of the Japanese 'Ogasawara-Shotō'
main island of the Bonin/Ogasawara archipelago, administered as the seat of a subdistrict of Tokyo, Japan
description_wiki: Chichijima (Japanese: 父島) is the largest and most populous of the Bonin Islands.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
surface_forms: atomic bomb was dropped on Hiroshima
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.

description: large canal in Panama
description_wiki: The Panama Canal (Spanish: Canal de Panamá) is an artificial 82-kilometer (51-mile) waterway in Panama that connects the Caribbean Sea with the Pacific Ocean.

description: sea port on the Caribbean Sea (Atlantic) coast of Panama
description_wiki: Colón (Spanish pronunciation: [koˈlon]) is a city and seaport in Panama, beside the Caribbean Sea, lying near the Atlantic entrance to the Panama Canal.

description: cape in the United States
description_wiki: Cape Hatteras  is a cape located at a pronounced bend in Hatteras Island, one of the barrier islands of North Carolina.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: river in New York State, United States
description_wiki: The Hudson River is a 315-mile (507 km) river that flows from north to south largely through eastern New York state.

description: president of the United States from 1945 to 1953; politician (1884–1972)
description_wiki: Harry S. Truman  (May 8, 1884 – December 26, 1972) was the 33rd president of the United States, serving from 1945 to 1953.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Norfolk (locally   NOR-fək) is an independent city in the U.S. commonwealth of Virginia.

description: president of the United States from 1981 to 1989 and actor (1911–2004)
description_wiki: Ronald Wilson Reagan (February 6, 1911 – June 5, 2004) was an American politician and actor who served as the 40th president of the United States from 1981 to 1989.
